title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: idaifieldR yet CRAN. Install GitHub using remotes:

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: follow along tutorial, load test dataset bundled package new Field Desktop project called rtest. import file located inst/testdata/rtest.jsonl GitHub. Create connection object connect_idaifield(): can also ping database see connection succeeds: Field Desktop running computer, serverip argument can left default ("localhost"). pwd argument password set Field Desktop Tools > Settings > password. Load complete project database R: result named list class idaifield_docs — one element per resource, named identifier, reflecting original JSON structure. Loading whole database can slow memory-intensive large projects; see Queries section targeted approach.
code: conn <- connect_idaifield(pwd = "hallo", project = "rtest") idf_ping(conn) idaifield_test_docs <- get_idaifield_docs(connection = conn)

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: index maps UUIDs human-readable identifiers used simplify_idaifield() make relations readable. Generate get_field_index(): verbose = TRUE index also includes shortDescription. gather_trenches = TRUE Place column added, contains nearest Place-category resource (). Use head(index) inspect , plot quick overview dataset:
code: index <- get_field_index(conn, verbose = TRUE, gather_trenches = TRUE) index |>   ggplot(aes(y = Place, fill = category)) +   geom_bar() +   labs(title = "Resources by context and category", y = "Place", x = "Count")

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: get_idaifield_docs() returns raw nested structure comes API, containing complete revision history resource. can reduce resource level maybe_unnest_docs(): step optional — idaifieldR functions handle idaifield_docs idaifield_resources lists. unnested version takes less memory easier browse RStudio.

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: simplify_idaifield() transforms nested list structure something R-friendly: Relations flattened named vectors relation.-prefix (e.g. relation.liesWithin) UUIDs relations replaced human-readable identifiers Geometry kept GeoJSON string keep_geometry = TRUE Single level lists flattened vectore, complex fields preserved -. need supply index complete project database , even working subset — used lookup table UUID replacement. also need supply project config: result class idaifield_simple. connection project name stored attributes carried later use. Check attributes(idaifield_test_simple).
code: config <- get_configuration(conn) idaifield_test_simple <- simplify_idaifield(   idaifield_test_resources,   index        = index,   config       = config,   keep_geometry = TRUE )

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: Use idf_select_by() filter simplified list field:

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: Alternatively, query database directly instead loading everything first. useful large projects need small subset: See also ?idf_index_query ?idf_json_query targeted queries.
code: pottery_docs <- idf_query(conn, field = "category", value = "Pottery")  # Always supply the index of the complete database when simplifying a subset pottery_simple <- simplify_idaifield(pottery_docs, index = index, config = config, keep_geometry = TRUE)

title: How to use idaifieldR: Detailed Workflow
text: Convert simplified list matrix data frame idaifield_as_matrix(): , standard R ggplot2 workflows apply:
code: pottery_df <- idaifield_as_matrix(pottery) |>   as.data.frame() pottery_df |>   ggplot(aes(x = relation.liesWithin, fill = period.start)) +   geom_bar() +   labs(title = "Pottery by context and period",        x = "Context", y = "Count") +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) pottery_df |>   ggplot(aes(x = vesselForm, fill = specificType)) +   geom_bar() +   labs(title = "Pottery by vessel form and type",        x = "Vessel form", y = "Count") +   theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

title: Importing Data from Field Desktop / iDAI.field into R
text: demonstration package uses included paper “R wie : Reproduzierbar. Auswertung von Daten aus Field Desktop mit idaifieldR” published Forum Digital Archaeology Infrastructure (2023, DOI: 10.34780/068b-q6c7) corresponding supplement. Alternatively, can check Demo.Rmd-vignette (browseVignettes("idaifieldR")) TLDR.Rmd-vignette shorter overview. First, connect “rtest” project using connect_idaifield() retrieve index resources using get_field_index(). Next, import resources database single list lists using get_idaifield_docs(). provides metadata changes users, can slow use lot memory. Alternatively, can query database directly using idf_query() retrieve resources need, resources “pottery” category. importing data, use simplify_idaifield() transform nested list usable format, replacing UUIDs identifiers converting dating fields minimum maximum values. configuration available, variables checkbox-fields also converted multiple columns boolean values. Lastly, can use idaifield_as_matrix() produce matrix row represents resource column represents field checkbox value. matrix can easily converted data.frame analysis:
code: library(idaifieldR) # Connect to iDAI.field: conn <- connect_idaifield(pwd = "hallo", project = "rtest")  # Get an index of the projects database: index <- get_field_index(conn)  # Get all docs from the projects database: idaifield_docs <- get_idaifield_docs(conn)  # Get only the docs of category "Pottery" from the database: pottery <- idf_query(conn, field = "category", value = "Pottery")  # Simplify the nested list:  pottery <- simplify_idaifield(pottery, uidlist = index)  # Convert it into a matrix: pottery_mat <- idaifield_as_matrix(pottery)  # Look at the result: View(pottery_mat)
